'''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' is open for edits by anyone; add entrances/slide exits as long as it follows site rules and fits the page. Happy editing! ''It is '''extremely''' encouraged to help [{{fullurl:{{FULLPAGENAME}}|action=edit}} expand it!]{{#if:{{{1|}}}|

{{Class 3|color-h=260|color-s=90%|color-l=50%|security=Unstable|entity=Medium Entity Count}} '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' is the 196th level of the Backrooms.
==
Description
== {{Blurimage|file=Abu_Dhabi_national_aquarium_amazonas_500_orphek_reef_aquarium_led_lighting-scaled.webp|caption=The most famous image taken of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'''; the photographer's status is unknown.}} {{Blurimage|file=Aquarium.webp|caption=An image of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'''}} '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''', informally known as Aquafina, is an expansive old aquarium from the mid-90s. The aquarium consists of a series of disorganized hallways expanding in various directions — including various transparent containers which are composed of materials ranging from glass or acrylic, and essential life-support systems filters, heaters, lighting, and substrate to house aquatic creatures like fish, invertebrates, and plants. The walls appear in violet-concrete colors, whereas the floor is often covered with varying tiles, along with an expansive carpet — strangely comprised of dried seaweed — surfacing the majority of the floor. The structure of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' is relatively unstable, with objects and decorations shifting to various positions and the tendency for the layout to inexplicably shift and change. Hallways branch down to numerous directions and/or loop back into themselves, causing confusion among wanderers. Additionally, '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' possesses a phenomenon for the decorations and objects to shift/change which are often being minor, such as the carpet color slightly changing or an water bottles shifting to the floor. Doors that should lead to the outside of the restaurant will instead lead seamlessly into further iterations of themselves; others open to different rooms such as '''Gift Shops''' or '''Slide Rooms'''. Fluorescent lighting is located above the aquarium, along with LED lights strewn across the level, which are responsible of the majority of the light source in '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'''. The lighting system of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' is relatively poor, habitually flickering and sometimes shutting off entirely. Furthermore, these panels negatively impact one's mental health, as staring into the light for extended periods may cause mental disorientation among wanderers. The lighting is unique throughout the level, as the dimmer the section is, the colderTemperatures range from a chilling 5°C to warm 25°C, though the waters located throughout the aquarium is observed at subzero temperatures. despite the subzero temperatures, the water is unable to freeze. the section it is and vice versa. Wanderers speculate that the lighting is the primary heat source of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''', though it remains unconfirmed. {{Blurimage|file=National-Aquarium-at-al-Qana.webp|caption=A close-up of a fish tank sitting on a table.}} The aquarium walls consist of stratified glass panes formed from an unidentified material, making them unbreakable against any damage and also preventing flood into the entire level. Aquariums feature self-sustaining aquatic biomes of sedimented sand and underwater plants submerged in lukewarm water that support small marine creatures such as fish and rays, along with aquatic life such as sea corals. Additionally, underwater replicas of carved structures and eroded columns are submerged in water, along with decorative fixtures and oxygen tanks. Air conditioners are present in a relatively decent condition. It releases an cold breeze where that is responsible for part of the indoor temperature, with various particles such as oxygen being released by the air conditioner. Water vapor is also released by the air conditioners, causing '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' to be more humid than other levels. Additionally, a uniform of pipes line the walls with them including various substances, such as oxygen gas and water which supplies the aquarium. The pipes emit a moderately rumbling sound, reminiscent of those found in [[Level 4]], which enhances the ambience throughout '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'''. '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' includes various technological features, such as Wi-Fi and television. Wi-Fi is always available for wanderers with a requirement to enter a password, where the password may range from 4-10 characters long. The password to the Wi-Fi of '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}''' is written on unique violet papers which are scattered throughout the level. The Wi-Fi has relatively poor signal and disconnects every 4 hours, though function as usual. TVs from the 90s and above can also be found attached to the walls. Although they function quite similarly to TVs in '''The Frontrooms,''' the channels that they offer are known to change every so often. Sometimes these TVs can be out of service, either due to them being under maintenance, or being replaced by newer models. ==
The Gift Shop
== {{Blurimage|file=Aquarium-0004-1.jpg|caption=An image of the Gift Shop}} '''The Gift Shops''' an boundless complex of shelves and aisles including varying features one may expect to find in typical gift shop. While abundant, the products on '''The Gift Shops''''s shelves are bereft of anything useful — with most of the supplies are in abysmal quality, the most prominent danger within the level being a stark lack of available useful resources. '''The Gift Shop''''s overhead fluorescent lighting emits a loud buzz, with many individuals going through extended stays experiencing a sense of discomfort. Littered across the floor are a wide variety of questionable items, ranging from [[Level 10#Entities|questionably sourced]] live worms to [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], among other things. The electricity within '''The Gift Shops''' is predictably poorly maintained—with blackouts being abundant in occurrence, though it is suggested that this may be the result of [[Phenomenon 12|external causes]]. Due to this, vast sections of the area are frequently without power for extended periods of time, often for days at a time. It is noted that Wi-Fi does not work in this section, and devices will automatically lose their signal if they are connected to Wi-Fi. ==
The Slide Room
== {{Blurimage|file=IMG_3840.png|caption=An image of '''The Slide Room'''}} '''The Slide Room''' is an indoor abandoned waterpark with a variety of slides. Unlike the rest of the area, it is dimly-lit by a purple hue, envoking an eerie sensation. The ceiling is made out of various concrete tiles, the floor is made out of a variety of wooden boards and concrete with them occasionally being flooded by lukewarm water. {{Blurimage|file=3990.png|caption=Another image of a park within '''The Slide Room'''.}} The section is heavily decorated with artificial elements that attempt to mimic the aesthetics of a tourist water park. Plastic palm trees, potted flowers, chairs, and beach items such as umbrellas can be found. Fake sea animals can also be found floating randomly on the water; the vast majority are dolphins, while others are toads and seals. All of this gives the level a cheerful and nostalgic feel. These objects give the level a cheerful, holiday-like appearance, but at the same time generate a feeling of artificial nostalgia in most explorers. The water in the pools on this level varies considerably in composition. Some pools have been found to contain moderate concentrations of chlorine, similar to those found in normal swimming pools. However, other pools are completely devoid of this compound, which has allowed the proliferation of various bacteria and microorganisms, especially the [[Hydrolitis Plague]] and, in rare cases, cholera. A unique property of the section is the slides. These slide are composed in approximately 94 color variations, specifically rare slides may send one to various level. As such, only a small portion of these slides are documented. In addition, these slides may fade or shift to varying positions, rarely glitching to each other. These slides are composed of durable plastic in decent quality, with only a handful of these slides having severe damages. Below are the listed slides:
*The yellow slide leads to [[Level 0]]. If the slide dips and leads straight down, it will lead to [[The Basement]] * The clear-ish green slide leads to [[Level 13]] * The dark yellow slide leads to [[Level 0.7]] * The brown-ish red slide leads to [[Level 245]] *The orange slide leads to [[Level 121]] *the metal slide leads to [[level 41]] *The grey slide leads to [[Level 483]] *The brown slide leads to [[Level 27]] *The green slide leads to [[Level 83]] *The red slide leads to [[Level !]] *The blue slide leads to [[Level 37]] *the glass (filled with water) slide actually loops back to the start of the level, making this slide pointless. *The black and white slides are infested with [[Entity 3|Smilers]] blocking the path, therefore it is not recommended to go through these slides.
==
Entities
== Within deeper and dimly-lit regions or within the slides of the '''Slide Rooms''', [[Entity 3|Smilers]] can be hiding in messy and narrow areas, waiting to ambush any living beings they come upon. Encountering a [[Entity 9|Faceling]] is a rare moment one could be in, and it had been recorded that majority of them are hostile. Addionally, there are gossiped rumors about [[Entity 10|Skin Stealers]] roaming around obscured corners of the '''Slide Room''', though these remain unconfirmed as of yet. ==
Colonies & Outposts
== There is one outposts located within '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'''. === M.E.G. Outpost Aquamarine ===
*Consists of 12 [[M.E.G.]] personnel. *Friendly but not open to trading. *They reside in the level for researching purposes, including documenting various anomalies that are located within the level.
==
Entrances & Exits
== === Entrances ===
*Entering the code “Alom Water” in an [[Object 1|Almond Water]] store in [[Level 11]] leads here; *Noclipping on a purple building in [[Level 118]] leads to this level.
=== Exits === The only exit is venturing through '''{{FULLPAGENAME}}'s''' waterslides. {{Footnotes}}
